params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: purpose
params.description: Filter queries by the file purpose. If you don't provide a purpose, the queries return unfiltered files.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

items.docs: Returns a list of the files that your account has access to. Stripe sorts and returns the files by their creation dates, placing the most recently created files at the top.

To upload a file to Stripe, you need to send a request of type multipart/form-data. Include the file you want to upload in the request, and the parameters for creating a file.

All of Stripe’s officially supported Client libraries support sending multipart/form-data.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves the details of an existing file object. After you supply a unique file ID, Stripe returns the corresponding file object. Learn how to access file contents.
